mnemonics: Think of intuitive security like a house: Lock doors (authentication), draw curtains (privacy), and don't leave keys under the mat (obvious flaws).
keyPoints: Intuitive methods prioritize user-friendly security that feels natural and is easy to adopt.
They aim to prevent security fatigue by integrating protection seamlessly into common workflows.
Effectiveness relies on the method being genuinely secure, not just feeling secure to the user.
commonMistakes: Confusing a 'good feeling' with actual security, leading to a false sense of protection.
Over-simplifying a complex security requirement to make it intuitive, thereby creating vulnerabilities.
